lenses.guidance: The locust-and-darkness pairing (vv 1-20, 21-29) inverts creation imagery — locusts darken the land (v 15), then darkness 'that may be felt' (v 21). The narrative structure rolls back Genesis 1's separation of light from darkness.

Three days of darkness (vv 22-23) directly attack Ra, Egypt's chief deity. The covenant arc dismantles the Egyptian pantheon plague by plague, preparing the people for the redemption Passover will deliver.

'Darkness over the land' (vv 21-23) anticipates Amos 8:9, Joel 2:31, and the three-hour darkness at the cross (Mt 27:45). A canonical pattern: judgement precedes redemption; the deepest dark is closest to the dawn.
